title: Grow basil hydroponically
description: Learn how to grow plants without soil!

dialogue.text: Hey there! I'm Hydro, your local hydroponics expert! Before you ask, it's just a nickname :P

options.text: Hydroponics? That sounds really interesting, tell me more.

dialogue.text: Oh I could talk for hours but I'll give you the short version: hydroponics is a method of growing plants using mineral nutrient solutions in water without soil. It's a great way to save resources and space while ensuring a constant supply of fresh produce during your space adventures. Unless you're growing outside, you'll definitely incur a bit of energy costs from indoor lighting, but on the flip side you can save up to 95% on your water usage compared to traditional farming. I've got a hydroponics tub for you to get started if you're interested.

options.text: That sounds amazing! Let's get started.

dialogue.text: Excellent! First things first, I need you to accept this hydroponics tub. It's all set up, you just need to add water and the plant seeds to get started.

options.text: Ooh, fancy!
I've got the hydroponics tub, what's next?

dialogue.text: Now that you've got your hydroponics tub, you'll need some dechlorinated water. There are a few techniques to accomplish this, but the simplest and cheapest way is to just let tap water sit outdoors for 48 hours or so. Here, take this sink!

options.text: Let that sink in!
Kind of weird that I didn't have one of those already.

dialogue.text: Good call. Now that your sink is installed, fill a bucket with tap water and we can prep it for the hydroponics tub.

options.text: No, I think I've got this one, thanks!
Alright, I've completed the challenging task of filling a bucket with water. What's next?

dialogue.text: Next you dechlorinate, of course! You may have to scoop some bugs out after, but this is a decent way to ensure there's no chlorine. Just make sure it gets direct sunlight!

options.text: It's like watching paint dry...
My water should be good to go now, right?

dialogue.text: Perfect, that's exactly what we needed. Next we'll make seedlings from scratch so you can learn the full workflow. Start by soaking 10 starter plugs for about 15 minutes so they're evenly hydrated. Rockwool holds moisture well, which makes it great for hydroponics.

options.text: BRB gonna grab a coffee...
The plugs are soaked and ready. Time to plant?

dialogue.text: Exactly. Drop a seed or two into each starter plug, keep them moist, and give them light. In about a week you should have 10 healthy basil seedlings ready to transplant.

options.text: I sure hope I did this right...
Great news: the seedlings came in strong. Ready for the tub transfer.

dialogue.text: Welcome back! Your seedlings look great. Let's move all 10 into the hydroponics tub with fresh dechlorinated water and nutrients. I've got nutrients ready for you now.

options.text: Nutrients, eh? I'll take 'em!
Alright, time to get my hands dirty!
Ok, I've filled the tub with water and nutrients. What's next?

dialogue.text: Now for the grow phase. Basil needs steady light, so let's use a dedicated grow lamp. This one draws 36 dWatt per hour, and the full grow takes about 4 weeks, so check the Energy page before you start. Also prep one extra bucket of dechlorinated water now so your post-harvest refresh is ready.

options.text: Cool grow lamp! This'll add a nice pink hue to my room.
4 weeks?? Geez!
Thanks for the heads up! I'd hate to have to wait an extra 48 hours.
A month older and a month wiser. I've got a bunch of basil now! Is it time to harvest?

dialogue.text: Beautiful work! Those basil plants are harvest-ready. Snip the tops to encourage bushier regrowth, and try not to remove more than one-third of the foliage at once so photosynthesis stays strong.

options.text: This basil is a cut above the rest, if I do say so myself! Snippity snip!
I've got these fresh bundles of basil! How do they look?

dialogue.text: They look perfect! You could sell them, cook with them, or even munch on them like a rabbit if you're so inclined! Just make sure you wash them first! No telling what's been floating around your room over the last 4 weeks! I'm sure you're eager to get started on your next batch, and that's relatively simple. Just replace your nutrient-deficient water with a fresh batch of water nutrients, and you can start the process all over again! I know it feels a bit wasteful, but don't worry, I've got big plans to address that! Plus, you're not growing from scratch, so another 2 weeks should do it! Feel free to start the growth process and finish this quest whenever you're ready!

options.text: What is old is new again!
Out with the old, in with the new! I'm ready to start growing again!
Thanks for all your help and advice, Hydro! You're the best! Don't tell the others I said that.
